title: NEO 3.1-C Human-Gate Ledger schema (the authority AS4 binds an UNLOCK_RECORD to)
description: Schema for .neo/gates/HUMAN_GATE_LEDGER.json. The ledger is the AUTHORITY: an UNLOCK_RECORD's gate_ref must resolve to a HUMAN entry here (gate_kind in {human_end_keep, human_start_approval}, authorized_by a human not a role) or AS4 FAILS. Resolved by verify_app_slice.ps1 from the NEO script root (the app tree cannot supply it); an operator/harness may override the path with -HumanGateLedger for fixtures. Append-only. PROVISIONAL-DEV: in dev this is a plain repo file the session principal can write, so it is tamper-EVIDENT not tamper-PROOF; it becomes un-forgeable only when section 4.9 (S5) puts it under a host-held root-of-trust + Option A (S6) ACLs it outside NEO's write scope.

root_of_trust.description: 'provisional-dev' in dev (tamper-evident); 'host-anchored' only once S5/S6 place it outside the principal's write scope.

gate_ref.description: Convention: keyed to a roadmap section 8 decision-log id, e.g. '2026-06-30-P1-S3-END-KEEP'.

gate_kind.description: The human gate that discharged this. A role/agent self-grant is not representable.

authorized_by.description: The HUMAN principal (e.g. 'Raphael'). A role/agent value is rejected by AS4.

app_slug.description: Which app this gate authorizes touches in.

authorized_paths.description: Exact repo-relative locked paths this gate authorizes. AS4 requires every touched denylisted path to be named here.

decision_log_ref.description: Human-readable cross-reference to the roadmap section 8 decision-log entry.
